database.name: False Positive Database
database.description: Common patterns that look like vulnerabilities but are usually not exploitable without additional evidence

entries.description: Server error responses that do not leak internal paths, stack traces, or user data
entries.reason: Requires proof of sensitive information disclosure or denial of service impact
entries.control: Check response body for internal paths, credentials, or user data

entries.pattern: Internal hostname in response without reachable service
entries.description: Response contains internal server names or Kubernetes pod identifiers that cannot be accessed externally
entries.reason: No exploit path without network access to internal infrastructure
entries.control: Attempt to reach the hostname externally; check for SSRF or DNS rebinding vectors

entries.pattern: CORS header without credentialed readable response
entries.description: Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * or specific origin but response contains no sensitive data or requires authentication
entries.reason: CORS misconfiguration requires sensitive data in credentialed response
entries.control: Test with credentials (cookies, Authorization header) and check for sensitive data in response

entries.description: Different response lengths for different inputs without confirming vulnerability type
entries.reason: Size difference is a signal, not a vulnerability; requires classification
entries.control: Analyze response content, timing, error messages to classify the anomaly

entries.pattern: CSRF token expiry interpreted as auth bypass
entries.description: Request fails with stale CSRF token, succeeds with fresh token
entries.reason: CSRF tokens are designed to expire; this is expected security behavior
entries.control: Test with fresh token; check if token is tied to session or can be reused cross-user

entries.pattern: JWT that decodes but signature unverified
entries.description: JWT payload is readable (base64) but signature validation is not tested
entries.reason: Decoding is not exploitation; must prove signature bypass or algorithm confusion
entries.control: Test algorithm switching (RS256->HS256), empty signature, or key confusion

entries.description: Automated scanner reports a finding that cannot be manually verified
entries.reason: Scanner false positives are common; manual reproduction required
entries.control: Follow scanner evidence path; craft manual request matching scanner behavior

entries.description: GraphQL endpoint returns schema via introspection query
entries.reason: Introspection is often intended for API discovery; rarely a security issue in production
entries.control: Check if introspection is disabled in production or if sensitive operations are exposed

entries.pattern: Version disclosure in headers or error pages
entries.description: Server or framework version visible in Server header, X-Powered-By, or error messages
entries.reason: Version alone is not exploitable; requires known CVE for that specific version
entries.control: Cross-reference version with CVE databases; test for known vulnerabilities

entries.description: Absence of X-Frame-Options, Content-Security-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, etc.
entries.reason: Missing headers are hardening recommendations, not vulnerabilities without exploit proof
entries.control: Demonstrate concrete attack (clickjacking, XSS) that the header would prevent
